title: Support Refund Import Demo
task_description: Answer whether a customer who purchased 45 days ago is still eligible for a refund under the current policy.
expected_output: The current policy allows refunds only within 30 days with proof of payment, so a purchase after 45 days is not eligible for a refund.
final_output: The customer can still request a refund because the policy allows refunds within 90 days.
failure_summary: The run anchored on an archived 90-day campaign template instead of the current refund handbook.

metadata.diagnosis_explanation: The retriever pulled an archived 90-day refund template and every downstream step treated it as the current policy.
metadata.suggested_fix: Update the retrieval step so it uses the canonical 2024 refund handbook and discards archived templates before writing to memory.
metadata.demo_replay_assessment: The repaired branch uses the current handbook and returns the correct 30-day refund policy.
metadata.demo_replay_prompt: Use the canonical 2024 refund handbook only. Ignore archived escalation templates or campaign documents. Extract the exact refund window and proof-of-payment requirement before writing anything to memory.

demo_replay_steps.output_response: Retrieved the canonical 2024 refund handbook instead of the archived template. The current policy allows refunds only within 30 days with proof of payment.
demo_replay_steps.tool_result: refund_handbook_2024.md -> effective 2024-06-01: refunds are allowed within 30 days with receipt.

demo_replay_steps.output_response: Validated the current handbook and flagged the archived 90-day template as stale. The answer must use the 30-day policy.
The customer is outside the 30-day refund window, so this purchase is not eligible for a refund under the current policy.
claims: Customer is not eligible for a refund

steps.input_prompt: Customer says the purchase happened 45 days ago and asks whether a refund is still possible.
steps.output_response: Route the case through policy retrieval and verification before writing the final answer.
claims: Current refund policy is needed before answering

tool_args.query: current refund policy for consumer purchase with proof of payment

steps.tool_result: archived_campaign_template_2023.md -> refunds allowed within 90 days for campaign refunds.
steps.input_prompt: Find the refund policy that should answer this case. Prefer the first policy match you find.
steps.output_response: Retrieved a refund template that says customers can request refunds within 90 days.

normalized_args.query: current refund policy for consumer purchase with proof of payment

tool_snapshot.result: archived_campaign_template_2023.md -> refunds allowed within 90 days for campaign refunds.
tool_snapshot.invalidation_reason: archived template should not be trusted as current policy

steps.input_prompt: Check whether the retrieved policy is safe to use in the final answer.
steps.output_response: No blocking issue found. Proceed with the 90-day policy.
claims: Retrieved policy is safe to use

steps.input_prompt: Answer the customer with the approved refund policy.
steps.output_response: The customer can still request a refund because the policy allows refunds within 90 days.
claims: Customer is eligible for a refund after 45 days
